# RFC-0007: Runtime Image Production Contract

> **Version:** 0.1.0 | **Status:** normative | **Phase:** test

---

## 1. Summary

### [RFC-0007:C-SUMMARY] Summary (Informative) <a id="rfc-0007c-summary"></a>

This RFC governs runtime image production: building validated OCI images from the same workspace baseline that serves host runs, content-closure reuse, recipe-referenced validation, and artifact landing. [RFC-0001](../rfc/RFC-0001.md) holds the specification overview and the authority map.

*Since: v0.1.0*

---

## 2. Specification

### [RFC-0007:C-IMAGE-BUILD] Runtime Image Production Boundary (Normative) <a id="rfc-0007c-image-build"></a>

Inferlab MUST support runtime-image production as a workflow separate from the long-running server and closed-loop recipe lifecycles.

An image definition MUST identify a reproducible runtime-image production unit within the workspace revision, declaring its serving environment selection, locally built package selection, base image, target hardware platform batch, and model validation coordinates. Runtime-image production MUST support multiple target platforms and multiple model validation coordinates in one invocation. Model validation coordinates MAY be empty for a build-only image.

Each model validation coordinate MUST reference a workspace recipe and optional case selection. An image definition MUST NOT restate server or measurement configuration that its referenced recipes already own.

Runtime-image production MUST use the workspace source state, Pixi dependency locks, integration selection, local builder bindings, and evidence facilities defined by this specification where those facts apply.

A runtime-image build MUST reject a dirty workspace.

Every referenced definition, recipe coordinate, and builder binding MUST resolve before the first external effect. Inferlab MUST create a durable local execution record before the first external effect of a non-dry-run image build.

The public runtime CLI MUST provide `image build` accepting an image identifier. `image build` MUST execute resolution, assembly, inspection, requested export, and eligible validations as one closed-loop workflow.

A non-dry-run `image build` MUST report the record identity on the operator diagnostic stream as soon as the record exists, and MUST report each package build, assembly, inspection, export, and validation phase as it begins, including the durable log path where one exists. Machine-readable output MUST remain a single final report.

`image build` MUST support dry-run mode. Image dry-run MUST report each target platform, the resolved image content closure identity, the assembly deduplication plan, resolution observations, and each validation coordinate's eligibility classification. Image dry-run MUST NOT assemble an image, export an artifact, launch a validation server, execute environment checks, or create a record that claims execution occurred.

Inferlab MUST generate the image build context from resolved workspace facts: the committed Pixi lock, locally built framework packages, and the resolved base image. Image production MUST NOT require the workspace to author container build files. The build context MUST be frozen before assembly begins and MUST contain only the generated build files, the declared environment check and postprocess scripts copied from the workspace, and the packaged artifacts the image consumes; package-build scratch, sanitized source copies, and durable logs MUST live outside the build context, and failure to remove the sanitized source copies MUST fail the assembly; a cleanup failure MUST be preserved in the assembly evidence even when a build, mutation, or audit failure is primary.

Image resolution MUST resolve the declared base image to an immutable per-platform image digest before assembly.

Image resolution MUST scope the assembly plan to the declared target platforms the selected builder can produce. An unproducible platform MUST be excluded before its content closure is derived and MUST be reported with a reason in dry-run output, the execution record, and the product manifest; exclusion is not a failure. When the selected builder can produce none of the declared platforms, resolution MUST fail.

An image definition MAY select the source-set paths built into wheels, including buildable subpackage paths; when the selection is omitted, every source-set path is built. Source paths consumed only at package build time remain build inputs without producing their own wheel.

Before building any required package, a non-dry-run image build MUST execute the declared environment checks of the selected serving environment against the local workspace realization ([RFC-0002:C-ENVIRONMENT-CHECKS](../rfc/RFC-0002.md#rfc-0002c-environment-checks)). A failed entry check MUST abort the build before any package build begins, MUST present the declared repair hint, MUST finalize the record, and MUST still emit the invocation's single machine-readable failed report; an infrastructural failure while executing entry checks MUST also finalize the record. The abort is cheap by design: the local realization and the image share one locked closure, so a local invariant failure predicts a doomed assembly, and package builds execute in that same local environment.

Runtime-image production MUST build required framework packages from the resolved workspace source state and MUST fail before assembly when a required package cannot be produced from that state. Production MAY reuse a previously built package only when its committed source identity, the selected environment's locked package closure, the content identity of editable packages installed outside the source set, the projected activation environment, generator and build-procedure identity, and target platform are all unchanged; reuse MUST be recorded. A built package MUST be published to the reuse cache atomically and immutably: staging MUST be private to the publishing invocation, a published artifact MUST never be overwritten, and a publication race MUST be resolved by adopting the already-published artifact and recording its content digest. After a successful assembly the build context MUST NOT retain package payloads; the reuse cache holds the bytes, and content digests with build logs remain the record's package evidence. Each locally built package included in a build context MUST be identified by content digest in the image record.

A locally built package MUST replace the identically named locked upstream package in the image content, so an image never mixes locally built sources with their lagging upstream releases.

Package builds MUST run in a clean process environment — only the composed activation, a fixed system-utility path suffix, and the sanitized-view redirects reach the build, so ambient shell state is never a build input — and MUST run against sanitized copies of the source-set trees, with build-time source references in the activation environment redirected into the sanitized view; an activation value carrying more than one workspace-root reference MUST be rejected rather than partially redirected. The activation projection MUST compose feature-scoped activation according to the selected Pixi environment's feature composition — earlier-listed features take precedence, a feature's target table overrides its untargeted table, and the default feature applies last unless the environment excludes it — and this precedence MUST be verified against the Pixi toolchain's observed behavior. Activation scripts that bind to the selected environment MUST be rejected at image resolution rather than silently omitted, because a script's exports are not statically knowable; scripts under features the environment does not compose are inert and MUST NOT be rejected. Inferlab MUST detect workspace source mutation caused by external build tooling after package builds, whether or not the build itself succeeded, MUST fail the assembly recording the mutated paths while preserving the build failure when both occurred, and MUST NOT silently clean or revert the workspace. A mutation audit that cannot execute MUST itself fail the assembly, preserving the build outcome alongside the audit failure in the assembly evidence.

Image assembly MUST execute the declared image-realization postprocess steps and then the declared environment checks inside the image build itself, after environment installation, through the image's entrypoint contract so the examined environment equals the runtime activation ([RFC-0002:C-ENVIRONMENT-CHECKS](../rfc/RFC-0002.md#rfc-0002c-environment-checks)). A failed in-image check MUST fail the assembly before an immutable image identity exists: builder storage, assembly reuse, export, and the product manifest MUST never observe an image whose declared checks did not pass. Image assembly MUST leave attributable per-check results in the durable builder log, and a failed assembly MUST still record which checks executed and how they exited; a check without recorded execution did not run.

The resolved image content closure MUST be derived from resolved input facts available before assembly: the workspace source identity of the required framework packages, the committed Pixi lock digest, the selected serving environment, the resolved base-image digest, the generated entrypoint contract, the declared environment check and postprocess script content, the generator identity, and the package build-procedure and context-generation procedure identities. The package build-procedure identity MUST enter both the package reuse key and the content closure, so a procedure change never reuses stale packages and never assigns one closure digest to differing package content. Built package content digests are execution evidence in the image record, not closure inputs. The closure MUST NOT incorporate builder hosts, workspace paths, model-weight locators, or model validation coordinates.

Model weights MUST remain external runtime inputs. An image build MUST NOT copy model weights or model-weight locators into its build context or output.

Builder hosts, machine names, local filesystem paths, and the machine-private facts known to resolution MUST NOT enter generated build-context text, image configuration, or OCI metadata, including image labels and annotations. This exclusion guarantee is scoped to generated text and image metadata: locally built binary payloads are protected by the sanitized-copy build rule rather than by text verification, and MAY embed paths of the sanitized build tree. Generated context text MUST be verified before assembly against the machine-private facts known to resolution and against host home-directory paths; activation values that would escape the entrypoint's quoting MUST be rejected. Activation variables whose names identify credential material MUST be excluded from the projected entrypoint and recorded as excluded; this name-based exclusion is best-effort protection for committed workspace content, whose freedom from secrets remains the workspace author's responsibility. Declared check and postprocess scripts copied into the context are committed workspace content under that same posture. Inferlab does not promise secret scanning of committed manifests, locks, or package URLs. The no-redaction evidence contract of this specification applies to local execution records; portable image artifacts MUST exclude machine-private facts from generated text and metadata by construction.

Local bindings MAY declare container environment pass-through variable names per machine. Declared names MUST be validated when bindings load: a name that is not a POSIX shell identifier, a duplicate, or a name Inferlab itself manages inside the container MUST be rejected — the launch scripts splice these names into shell parameter references, where anything beyond an identifier can carry expansion side effects, and a non-identifier name could not be referenced by the channel at all. A validation container MUST receive each declared variable by name reference only, so its value never enters the launch command line or the image content; a declared variable absent from the launching environment MUST be reported. The local execution record preserves the launch command as executed.

Local bindings MAY also declare per-machine container hardware facts for that same containerized substitution: host device paths, an unlimited pinned-memory ulimit, and Linux capabilities. These facts are operator-declared and MUST NOT be auto-detected. Declarations MUST be validated when bindings load: a device path that is not absolute, a duplicate device, a capability outside the fixed set Inferlab recognizes, or a duplicate capability MUST be rejected. A validation container MUST receive each declared device, the declared ulimit, and each declared capability; a containerized launch MUST NOT request privileged mode. Container hardware facts are runtime launch facts: they MUST NOT enter image content, content closures, or portable artifacts.

Within one `image build` invocation, requests with the same resolved image content closure and target platform MUST reuse one image assembly result even when multiple model compatibility validations consume it.

The immutable image identity MUST enter the assembly evidence the moment the builder returns it, so a later cleanup, inspection, or export failure cannot lose which image the assembly produced. Image inspection MUST establish and record the immutable image identity and the entrypoint contract of the built artifact.

Image validation MUST reuse recipe and case resolution, readiness, measurement, evidence, and cleanup semantics defined by this specification, with the built image substituting for the locally installed serving environment.

A model validation coordinate is eligible for a target platform if and only if that platform matches the selected local builder host, its recipe case resolves to single-host local placement, and its model weights are bound in local bindings. Eligible coordinates validate against that platform's assembled image.

An ineligible coordinate MUST be recorded as built-but-unvalidated rather than failed. A coordinate whose target-platform assembly failed MUST be recorded with a failed-assembly outcome rather than built-but-unvalidated.

A failed assembly or validation for one platform or coordinate MUST NOT prevent other requested platforms or coordinates from completing.

Builder selection MUST be deterministic. When local bindings declare more than one builder, the invocation MUST select one explicitly.

Built images MUST remain in local builder storage under a recorded immutable image identity by default. An operator MUST be able to request export to a local OCI archive. Each exported archive path MUST be unique to its producing record, so no repeated or concurrent build overwrites another record's artifact evidence. Configured artifact storage MAY receive exported artifacts.

The image-production workflow defined by this specification MUST stop at local or configured artifact storage and a product manifest. The image-production workflow MUST NOT push images to a registry.

The product manifest MUST record the mapping from each requested platform and validation coordinate to the produced image identity, the deduplication of equal content closures, produced artifacts, validation references, and complete or partial outcomes.

Image records MUST preserve build inputs, native builder commands, immutable image identity, inspection or export evidence, environment check outcomes for each examined realization, validation outcomes, partial failures, and cleanup outcomes when those facts apply. Read-only resolution probes (builder platform and base-image digest queries) are observations: each observed value MUST be recorded together with the exact command that produced it in the resolved plan, which dry-run reports and the durable record preserves. Every state-affecting builder command (assembly, inspection, export) MUST be persisted to the durable record before it executes, and the recorded command MUST be the executed command.

**Rationale:** Multi-platform and multi-model image output is an established product goal, but it does not require runtime serving and recipe execution to share a general operation DAG. A named image definition keeps image production a shareable workspace fact, recipe-referenced validation keeps one authority for server and measurement configuration, and a content closure derived only from generated, digest-pinned inputs makes deduplication verifiable while excluding weights and private facts by construction. Records stay honest locally while the generated text and metadata that leave the machine carry no machine-private facts: evidence is preserved where it is private, and excluded where it is portable. The portability promises are sized to what the tool can actually enforce — fail-closed verification for facts Inferlab knows, best-effort exclusion for committed content, sanitized views plus mutation detection instead of an unenforceable no-mutation absolute — because a guarantee the mechanism cannot honor is worse than a narrower one it can. Clean-only image inputs protect reproducible artifacts, and external weights with content-based reuse keep image outputs portable and avoid redundant assembly. Entry checks price a doomed build at seconds instead of an hour, and in-image checks executed as build steps mean an image that exists has passed its declared invariants — no unverified artifact can be cached, exported, or validated. Declared container hardware facts keep RDMA-dependent serving launchable in containers — pinned memory, InfiniBand and GPUDirect device nodes — while the operator states exactly what each host grants: no auto-detection to guess wrong, no privileged mode to grant everything, and no machine facts leaking into portable artifacts.

*Since: v0.1.0*
